newTests.name: `--eject-extension` bundles the embedded engine + worker with thin MV3 glue. > extension-files returns the full unpacked MV3 file set
`--eject-extension` bundles the embedded engine + worker with thin MV3 glue. > manifest.json is an MV3 manifest injecting into MAIN world with loopback perms

The engine survives a full-page navigation AND a browser restart: its connect
   route (url + token) is persisted per-origin in localStorage, so a real reload
   re-subscribes automatically instead of losing the tab. > persists the route, clears it on disconnect, and re-subscribes on reload

Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly. > scalar bridge results > renders a number scalar

The regular bridge NO LONGER auto-registers a service worker on connect — that
   responsibility moved to the browser EXTENSION (`spel bridge --eject-extension`).
   Connecting to the harness must not silently register /spel-sw.js. > connect() does not auto-register spel-sw.js (moved to the extension)

The connect dialog / popup / harness show the real spel brand mark (theatre
   masks + play triangle from README's logo.svg), not the placeholder emoji. > the extension popup + harness embed the brand mark, not a mask emoji
Tests for Playwright driver artifact resolution. > materializes artifacts from the resolved Maven jar

Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly. > map results still dispatch > renders :url map

The bridge token is stable across restarts (persisted to ~/.spel/bridge-token)
   so a once-ejected extension / bookmarklet stays authorized against every later
   bridge, instead of the old random-per-start token that caused 'unauthorized'. > persisted-token! honors a pre-existing token file (no rotation)

The bridge token is stable across restarts (persisted to ~/.spel/bridge-token)
   so a once-ejected extension / bookmarklet stays authorized against every later
   bridge, instead of the old random-per-start token that caused 'unauthorized'. > persisted-token! is stable across calls and written to disk

Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly. > map results still dispatch > renders :title map
Tests for Playwright driver artifact resolution. > resolves artifacts through tools.deps coordinates

The bridge tracks connected profiles (tabs) and exposes them so the connect
     dialog can show who is already connected. A real tab announces itself via
     the `/hello` POST; `/clients` lists it, and the dialog (`connected_profiles`)
   reads that listing. > collapses a reloaded tab into a single profile (no stale duplicate)

Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly. > map results still dispatch > keeps a nil JS-eval result silent

     dialog can show who is already connected. A real tab announces itself via

   reads that listing. > lists a connected tab's profile after the hello POST
